title: A 7.8 Quake Killed 37 in Mindanao. Tsunami Waves Followed.
script: Massive quake! On June 8, a 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Sarangani Province in southern Mindanao, Philippines. At least 37 people were killed, 482 were injured, and 4 are still missing. The shock is the strongest to hit the Philippines in 35 years. General Santos City, home to 722,000 people, took serious structural damage. Buildings collapsed in Digos and Kidapawan. Tsunami waves swept coastal towns for hours. Warnings were issued for Indonesia, Malaysia, Japan, Taiwan, and Papua New Guinea. Hundreds of aftershocks have continued since. Officials are now assessing whether seawall and building infrastructure — built decades ago — can handle the next big one. Is Mindanao's coastal infrastructure ready to survive what comes next?
